# flight-sim — {project_description}

<!-- PROJECT: Customize these principles for your domain. Principles 1-4 are suggested universals; 5-8 are workflow conventions. -->

### Core Principles

1. **Authoritative data source:** {describe your data authority pattern} — upstream tools author, downstream tools never corrupt.
2. **Non-destructive:** Original source files are never modified; all metadata, edits, and processing decisions stored externally.
3. **Reproducibility:** Every output can be reproduced exactly through preserved source files, metadata, derivatives, and operation logs.
4. **Orchestrate, don't replace:** The application delegates to specialized tools; it tracks state and guides the user.
5. **Maximize automation:** Auto-populate everything derivable; only creative/subjective decisions require user input.
6. **Protect expensive resources:** Pre-execution safety checks, cost tracking, and automation that prevents unnecessary reruns are top priority.
7. **Dual-path execution parity:** Every application action is executable via UI interaction or Assistant NL command; both paths converge on the same backend API with identical SSE-driven UI updates.
8. **Calibrated proactive assistance:** The system offers help calibrated to user experience and preferences; two dimensions: initiative level and interaction style, both tunable.

## CC's Role

CC's role is **development and implementation** of the application system. CC does not serve as a runtime application component — that role belongs to any assistant or automation layer built into the application.

CC responsibilities include: implementing API endpoints, building UI components, writing database migrations, creating CC task prompts, authoring knowledge documents, and executing pipeline operations.

## CC Safety Discipline

CC operates in two contexts: **Dev-session** (interactive terminal) and **Runtime** (server endpoints, scheduled ops). Universal rules apply in both.

### Core Rules

| Rule | Scope | Implementation |
|------|-------|----------------|
| Never modify original source files | Universal | All metadata writes go to sidecar or external stores; original source files are read-only |
| Never modify source records | Universal | Authoritative records (sensor data, camera EXIF, raw inputs) are a historical record — never overwritten |
| Reversible by default | Universal | File moves use quarantine; metadata writes use non-destructive mode; batch operations support dry-run |
| Copy-on-write for irreversible operations | Universal | If an operation cannot be undone, create a new file and preserve the source |
| Confirmation before bulk actions | Dev-session | Operations affecting more than 10 files require explicit user approval |
| Atomic intent execution | Runtime | When user confirms an intent, backend executes all file operations as a single unit |
| Audit trail | Universal | Dev-session: CC logs timestamp, command, file count, outcome. Runtime: application logs to audit tables |
| Never read credential files | Universal | NEVER directly read `api_keys.json` or any file containing API keys/secrets/tokens. Diagnose key issues via validation scripts that don't expose values. See `docs/standards/cc-safety-discipline.md` §Credential File Protection |

**Full details:** `docs/standards/cc-safety-discipline.md`

## Session Initialization

**Mode 1 — General resumption** ("continue", "what's next", "where are we"):
Read the latest checkpoint or pulse from `project-status/` using instance-aware file selection:
- **Colored instance** (green/yellow/purple): read the latest file whose filename contains this instance's color. If none found, fall back to the latest non-color-coded file. If none found, proceed with no prior context.
- **Standard session** (no color identifier): read the latest file regardless of color coding.
If pulse, quick read and proceed. If checkpoint, summarize and ask what's next.

**Mode 2 — Direct task** ("check completions", "assess X_V2_review.md", "write task prompt for X"):
Skip the checkpoint read entirely. Go straight to the requested work.

**Mode 3 — Skip init** ("skip init", "no init"):
Skip checkpoint read; wait for next instruction.

In all modes: do NOT pre-read docs — reference them on demand.

## Development Workflow

Split workflow: **Claude AI (browser)** for design/specs/review; **Claude Code (terminal)** for implementation/testing/file ops.

### Commands

**Tests:**
```powershell
TBD
```

**Build:**
```powershell
```

**Start server:**
```powershell
```

### Conventions

- Python code always in `.py` files — never inline `python -c` commands
- CC commits with descriptive message referencing task ID and issue numbers. **CC does NOT push** — operator pushes manually.
- After git commit, CC sends a push notification (notifies operator's device via ntfy):

```powershell
Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://ntfy.sh/1668651d-48ae-4104-b09e-f742b559e205" -Method Post -Body "{TASK-ID} completed [flight-sim]"
```

Applies to ALL CC-executed prompts: task prompts, compliance prompts, inline bug fixes, housekeeping, any ad-hoc command. Message body: TASK-ID if available, else the tab label slug, else just `"completed"`.

- CC task prompts must be **fully self-contained**. Never reference content in other prompt files by proxy — inline everything CC needs to execute.

**CC launch command:**
```powershell
$env:CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_TERMINAL_TITLE = "1"; $host.UI.RawUI.WindowTitle = "{label}"; claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opusplan
```

## Project Status Tracking

### Commands (in Claude AI)
- `status checkpoint` — Full status checkpoint (end-of-day or milestone)
- `status retro` — Engineering retrospective
- `status prompts` — Prompt effectiveness assessment
- `pulse` — Lightweight breadcrumb (~3 lines, quick session handoff)
- `check updates` — Update three CD-maintained status files per `docs/commands/check_updates.md`
- `check completions` — Run check-completions protocol for completed CC tasks
- `check compliance` — Review compliance report after CC verification
- `check coverage` — Diff design decisions against task inventory; report gaps (see Task Validation Architecture §11)
- `generate audit [for {spec}]` — Generate a cross-instance spec audit prompt for CD2; resolves spec from argument or current context (see Task Validation Architecture §15)
- `review audit` — Review CD2's spec audit report and incorporate findings

### Status File Location
- Full checkpoints: `project-status/flight-sim-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM.md` (standard) or `flight-sim-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM-{color}.md` (colored instance)
- Pulse: `project-status/flight-sim-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM-pulse.md` (standard) or `flight-sim-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM-{color}-pulse.md` (colored instance)
- Valid colors: `green`, `yellow`, `purple` — identifies the CD instance that authored the file
- **staleness_threshold_days:** 3 | **default_history_days:** 7

## Conventions

### File Naming
- Specs (review workflow): `{Name}_V{n}.md` — bumps at review boundaries
- Spec review findings: `{Name}_V{n}_review.md` — in `docs/specs/`, permanent
- CC task prompts: `docs/tasks/{name}_prompt.md`
- Pre-flight deviations: `docs/tasks/{name}_prompt_deviation.md`
- Task completions: `docs/tasks/{name}_completion.md`
- Compliance prompts: `docs/tasks/{name}_compliance_prompt.md`
- Compliance reports: `docs/tasks/{name}_compliance.md`
- Checkpoints: `project-status/flight-sim-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM.md` (standard) or `flight-sim-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM-{color}.md` (colored instance)
- Pulses: `project-status/flight-sim-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM-pulse.md` (standard) or `flight-sim-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM-{color}-pulse.md` (colored instance)

### File Provenance (docs/ only)
All files created under `docs/` include a provenance header block:
- **Created:** ISO 8601 timestamp
- **Source:** prompt file path (e.g., `docs/tasks/task_prompt.md`) or description if no prompt file (e.g., `CD session — "check updates" command`)
- Archive files additionally include **Archived from:** and **Purpose:**
- Does NOT apply to `src/`, `tests/`, `config/`, `scripts/` — use `git blame` for those.

### Git
- CC commits with task ID + issue refs in message. **CC does NOT push.**
- After git commit, CC sends push notification via ntfy (see Conventions above for command). Applies to ALL CC-executed prompts (task, compliance, inline, housekeeping). Use TASK-ID, tab slug, or `"completed"` as message body.

## spec-review Hybrid Mode

### Canonical Source Policy
- **Parts are canonical:** `*_part{N}.md` — where edits happen
- **Aggregate is artifact:** `*.md` — regenerated from parts via `scripts/assemble_spec.sh`
- **Manifest:** `*_manifest.json` — index that bridges parts → aggregate; see `docs/standards/spec-manifest-schema.md`

### Mode Options
- `--mode full` — Review single document (default for small specs)
- `--mode section` — Review parts only, no integration check
- `--mode hybrid` — Section review on parts + integration review on aggregate
- `--mode auto` — Auto-detect based on manifest presence (default)

### Additional Flags
- `--manifest <file>` — Explicit manifest path
- `--skip-integration` — Skip Phase 2 in hybrid mode
- `--skip-section` — Skip Phase 1 in hybrid mode

### Example Invocations
```bash
/spec-review docs/specs/Spec_V2_manifest.json
/spec-review --mode full docs/specs/Spec_V2.md
/spec-review --mode hybrid --skip-integration docs/specs/manifest.json
```

### Post-Fix Workflow
1. Apply fixes to canonical PART files (referenced in findings Location fields)
2. Run `./scripts/assemble_spec.sh manifest.json` to regenerate aggregate
3. Optionally re-run integration review only: `/spec-review --mode hybrid --skip-section manifest.json`

### Helper Scripts
- `scripts/assemble_spec.sh <manifest.json>` — Regenerate aggregate from parts
- `scripts/generate_manifest.sh <spec_name> <version> <part_files...>` — Create manifest from existing parts
- `scripts/token_estimate.py <file> [--lines START-end] [--budget N] [--recursive]` — Token estimation for context window planning

Load the referenced file **before** executing tasks that match the trigger.

| Domain | Reference File | Load When |

| Compaction Resilience (CRP) | `docs/standards/compaction-resilience-protocol-v1.md` | Multi-phase tasks, doc generation >500 lines, any task mentioning "CRP" |

| Conventions (full) | `docs/commands/conventions.md` | `/spec-review`, tiered review, check completions, writing CC task prompts, parallel CC safety, assistant architecture |

| Task Validation | `docs/specs/Task_Validation_Architecture_V1.md` | Writing validation prompts, audit prompts, conversation exports, coverage audits |

**Loading Protocol:** When a task matches a trigger, read the reference file FIRST. Multiple references may apply — load all that match.
